data.title: Klarna to be offered as DoorDash payment option
data.description:
Klarna (KLAR) and DoorDash (DASH) have teamed up to offer Klarna's flexible payment options to DoorDash customers, the companies said on Thursday.

In coming months, DoorDash (DASH) customers will be able to choose from Klarna's range of payment options when buying groceries, retail, or the DashPass Annual Plan on DoorDash's website or app.

Payment options will include:

  • Paying in full;
  • Pay in four equal interest-free installments; or
  • Pay Later, by choosing a date that aligns with the customer's paycheck schedule.

"Our partnership with DoorDash marks an important milestone in Klarna’s expansion into everyday spending categories,"  said David Sykes, chief commercial officer at Klarna (KLAR).

data.description:

data.title: Eyenovia falls on plans to seek reverse merger
data.description:
Shares of Eyenovia (NASDAQ:EYEN) traded lower on Thursday after the ophthalmic product developer announced a non-binding agreement to seek a reverse merger with Betaliq, Inc., a privately held pharmaceutical company based in Florida.

Per the terms of a non-binding letter of intent the company has signed, Betaliq equity holders are set to own about 84% of the combined entity, while Eyenovia (NASDAQ:EYEN) will own the rest.

The transaction is set to create a new publicly listed eye care company, valuing the merging parties at approximately $77M and $15M, respectively.

Eyenovia (NASDAQ:EYEN), a nano-cap pharmaceutical firm with a market capitalization of roughly $3.5M, fell ~18% in the morning hours after the announcement.

In January, the developer of the Optejet drug delivery platform announced plans to evaluate strategic alternatives to maximize shareholder value.

The proposed transaction combining the Optejet platform with Betaliq’s EyeSol drug delivery technology “is in the best interests of our company, our team members, patients, and shareholders,” said Michael Rowe, EYEN CEO.

data.title: Instacart launches new AI tools for advertisers to drive growth for brands
data.description:
Online grocery delivery platform Instacart (NASDAQ:CART) on Thursday introduced a suite of new AI-powered automation tools that would help advertisers boost campaign performance for brands.

The new tools include AI-powered landing pages, campaign recommendations, product library enhancements, and universal campaigns.

The company has explained the functions of the tools as follows:

Universal Campaigns: One campaign that works across multiple ad formats with a single budget, optimizing in real-time for specific objectives like sales maximization.

AI-Powered Landing Pages: Creates shoppable brand destinations with AI to generate product showcases and custom copy.

Recommendations in Ads Manager: Gives data-driven insights that can be implemented with "a single click" to boost ad campaign performance.

Enhanced Product Library: Streamlined tools for managing product information at scale.

The AI-generated landing pages are available for all CPG advertisers, and other tools are currently in pilot with select brands, with plans for broader availability in the coming months, the company said.

The U.S. is the leading market for advertising, with ad spending expected to reach $455.9B by 2025, according to a report from Publift, in which digital ad spending is expected to see an 8-10% growth at about $325B this year.

data.title: HubSpot gets initiated with Market-Perform rating at Bernstein
data.description:
Bernstein began coverage of HubSpot (NYSE:HUBS) with a Market-Perform rating and a $693 price target.

Shares of HubSpot dipped about 1% on Thursday.

Analysts led by Firoz Valliji said HubSpot is a cloud-based sales and marketing software platform focused on business-to-business customers in the small and midsize businesses, or SMB, segment. "Over the years, HubSpot has done a good job of expanding its productportfolio, adding new customers, and up-selling to existing ones, resulting in best-in-classmarket share gain. In the foreseeable investment horizon, revenue growth will remain thekey thesis driver for the stock," the analysts added.

The analysts think HubSpot can deliver higher growth than Street expectations, as the company is well-positioned to continue gaining market share. Over the long term, the benefit of share gain will likely diminish as — customer relationship management, or CRM, is a maturing market; and HubSpot’s revenue base grows. 

Valliji and his team added that HubSpot will then need to deliver on profitability, which they think the company can. Overall, the analysts are optimistic about HubSpot's fundamental story. However, they said that valuation is at the higher end of historical levels and at a premium to software peers because the company is viewed as a beneficiary of potential SMB upswing and due to the shift in investor preference toward low-capex software vendors. Thus, the analysts think that the upside potential is capped.

data.title: J.P. Morgan sees opportunities in QSR sector, upgrades CAVA on growth potential
data.description:
Stock market volatility and the still-attractive 4.3% yield in the 10-year Treasury note has provided some investment opportunities in the quick service restaurant (“QSR”) industry. Coupled with upward revisions to comparable store sales for many names in the sector and limited exposure to changes in U.S. trade policies, J.P. Morgan singles out several names in the sector to outperform versus peers.

Among those is CAVA Group (NYSE:CAVA) which was upgraded to Overweight from Neutral with a nod to the stock’s 32% decline over the past 30 days.

“CAVA has significant US white space for expansion from its already multi-market success, is generating FCF unusually early, and has considerable near-term operational and brand initiatives to drive both sales and profits,” J.P. Morgan analysts John Ivankoe and Rahul Krotthapalli’ research showed.

CAVA’s growth trajectory is amplified by investments in technology to optimize labor and make the restaurants easier to run; a differentiated loyalty program; strong new store openings that are closing the “awareness gap” with peers like Chipotle Mexican Grill (CMG); and new menu options including seafood. With these initiatives, J.P. Morgan sees CAVA’s (NYSE:CAVA) comparable store sales growing by 10% in Q1 and 7.2% in FY25, significantly more than Chipotle’s (CMG) much more modest +2.6% and +2.5%, respectively.

While Ivankoe and Krotthapalli maintain their neutral rating on Chipotle (CMG) and Brinker International (EAT), they would look to add to positions on further volatility. Chipotle (CMG) should see peak comparable sales in the second half of the year, with Q3 momentum “surprising” the pair as average unit volume (“AUV”) is expected to perform ahead of normal seasonality.

For the broader restaurant industry and economic landscape, Ivankoe and Krotthapalli pay particular attention to the expectations component of consumer confidence data, as a less optimistic consumer becomes more careful in their discretionary spending. According to the latest data compiled by the Conference Board, the February consumer expectations index dropped 9.3 points to 72.9. This is the first time since June 2024 the expectations index was below the threshold of 80 that signals a recession ahead.

On the labor front, talent sourcing and retention remain key for building strong brands and effectively scaling operations. The study shows that turnover trends are improving among those embracing increased technology but remain below scale for fast casual names that remain focused on attracting the “right” talent.

By demographics, food spending as a percentage of total spending is 15.6% for the bottom 20% of income consumers (4.7% at restaurants, 11% at grocery stores) and 11.3% for the top 20% (5.2% restaurants, 6.1% grocery). As Washington considers exemptions for some agricultural goods from tariffs on Mexico and Canada, its questionable as to tariffs’ effectiveness on food staples not able to be produced in the U.S. at a rate to sustain domestic consumption, especially as it would have disproportionate impact on the lower income consumer.

The upgrade is giving a boost to CAVA Group (NYSE:CAVA) with shares up 5.4%.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, and Dow seesaw higher as Fed-driven rally picks up steam
data.description:
The major averages seesawed on Thursday as they push higher after earlier falling as traders assess the Federal Reserve's growth and inflation outlooks.

Early on and the S&P 500 (SP500) was +0.2%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.2%, and the Dow (DJI) was +0.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 4 basis points to 4.20%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 2 basis points to 3.94%.

U.S. stocks pulled back from their session highs on Wednesday, but still ended solidly higher after Federal Reserve chair Jerome Powell suggested that inflation caused by tariffs would only be "transitory."

"Tariffs will raise inflation and then lower growth, so the pattern is not unexpected, and of course there is considerable uncertainty given erratic government policy," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Federal Reserve officials stuck to their projection of two 25-basis-point interest-rate reductions this year while baking in slower economic growth and higher inflation, according to the central bank's updated Summary of Economic Projections published on Wednesday.

"Our U.S. economists think that while the Fed may be anticipating a slowdown in the hard data, they are on hold until that evidence emerges," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

A clear majority of the FOMC now see risks tilted towards higher inflation but towards weaker growth and higher unemployment, Reid added. 

President Trump, however, said the Fed should cut interest rates as tariffs start to "ease" into the economy, after Fed Chair Jerome Powell said tariffs may delay further progress in bringing inflation to the 2% target. 

"The Fed is slowing the reduction of its balance sheet. This reflects the fact that liquidity demand is not slowing so aggressively, and should necessarily be seen as an easing," Donovan said.

Initial jobless claims rose slightly in the past week, in line with expectations, while U.S. current account deficit narrowed slightly in Q4, beating consensus.

The Fed balance sheet will land later in the day.

data.title: Accenture slumps after Q2 new bookings dip, amid earnings beat
data.description:
Update: The story was updated with stock price movement and analysts' comments.

Shares of Accenture (NYSE:ACN) tumbled about 8% on Thursday despite fiscal second quarter results beat estimates, while new bookings for the second quarter declined year-over year.

The company narrowed its revenue growth outlook for fiscal 2025.

For the fiscal second quarter ended Feb. 28, revenue rose 5% in U.S. dollar terms (8.5% in local currency) year-over-year to $16.7B, while GAAP EPS climbed 7% year-on-year to $2.82. Both top and bottom line numbers beat estimates.

"The trust and confidence in our unique strengths and capabilities is reflected in 32 clients with quarterly bookings greater than $100 million and we are very pleased to have another milestone quarter in Gen AI with $1.4 billion in new bookings," said Accenture Chair and CEO Julie Sweet.

The Irish company's Consulting revenues in the second quarter grew 3% (in U.S. dollars) year-over-year to $8.28B, while Managed Services revenues jumped 8% (in U.S. dollars) to $8.38B.

Americas was the top geographic market by revenue, generating $8.55B in the fiscal second quarter, growing 9% (in U.S. dollars) year-over-year.

Generative AI new bookings for the fiscal second quarter were $1.4B.

However, the company noted that new bookings for the second quarter of fiscal 2025 were $20.91B, declining 3% in U.S. dollars and flat in local currency, compared to the second quarter of fiscal 2024. Accenture's new bookings for the first quarter had risen by only 1% (in U.S. dollars) year-over-year to $18.70B.

Analysts at TD Cowen said that the quarterly report is “better than feared on growth amid recent stock pressure, but mixed aspects exist,” including weaker-than-expected margins, according to a report by Bloomberg.

Meanwhile, Vital Knowledge said that the results showed solid sales, although weak margins. The firm added that bookings “fell a tiny bit short,” according to Bloomberg.

Dividend: Accenture declared a $1.48 quarterly dividend, the same as the previous quarter's amount. The record date is April 10, and the dividend is payable on May 15.

Stock Buyback: During the second quarter, Accenture bought back or redeemed 4M shares, including about 2.4M shares repurchased in the open market, for a total of $1.4B. Accenture's total remaining share repurchase authority, at Feb. 28, was around $5B.

Outlook: Accenture expects third quarter revenue to be between $16.9B and $17.5B (midpoint at $17.2B), compared to consensus estimate of $17.22B.

Fiscal year 2025: The company narrowed its revenue growth to be in the range of 5% to 7% (midpoint at 6%) in local currency, compared to 4% to 7% previously, consensus revenue growth is 5.73%.

The company continues to expect foreign-exchange impact of about negative 0.5% on results.

Related stocks: Cognizant Technology Solutions (CTSH) fell 3%, EPAM Systems (EPAM) declined about 4%, while Infosys (INFY) dipped about 3% on Thursday.

data.title: Carlyle seeks buyer for Colombian oil producer for ~$1.5B - Reuters
data.description:
Carlyle Group (NASDAQ:CG) is seeking a buyer for its Colombian oil producer SierraCol for ~$1.5B, Reuters reported Thursday.

The private equity firm set up SierraCol in 2020 after buying assets from Occidental Petroleum, and production of 45K boe/day makes SierraCol the largest independent producer in Colombia; its free cash flow in the 12 months to September totaled $172M, and net debt was $511M last year.

Carlyle (NASDAQ:CG) has grown and sold several oil and gas companies during the past decade, including Neptune Energy in the North Sea and Assala Energy in Gabon.

However, Carlyle's (CG) purchase of assets in Italy, Egypt and Croatia from Energean to create a Mediterranean-focused oil and gas producer reportedly is in jeopardy as a crucial regulatory approval from Italian authorities remains outstanding.
